steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Use SOH for sine
steps.content: Sine is opposite over hypotenuse. From angle $x^\circ$, the opposite side is $63$ and the hypotenuse is $73$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The ratio $\frac{63}{73}$ uses the side opposite angle $x^\circ$ over the hypotenuse. The distractors use the other leg, give a value far too small for this triangle, or give a value greater than $1$, which sine cannot be for an acute angle.
